narrative: Global equities surge on Iran-US ceasefire breakthrough, with continental Europe leading (+1.28% DAX, +1.18% CAC). OMX30 enters Monday in established bull regime (+8.1% above 200-MA, +1.9% above 50-MA) supported by 1.094% weighted correlation bias and commodity strength (gold +3.40%, copper +0.96%). However, technical momentum flags: ADX at 8.6 (bearish-biased, below 18 threshold) reduces confidence to Medium; Stochastic K=82.1 is overbought; MACD remains below signal with negative histogram (-2.371). US 10Y yields jumped +54bp to 4.487%, benefiting banks but pressuring rate-sensitive tech. VIX compression (-5.77%) and oil weakness (-5.11%) suggest risk appetite but lack decisive trend conviction.
morningBrief: OMX30 primed for modest lift on geopolitical relief and strong commodity backdrop, but weak technical structure demands caution. Watch 3123 (yesterday close) as pivot; upside capped near 3180 (50-MA) with support at 3085 (lower Bollinger band). Industrials (28%) and banks (18%) are primary beneficiaries—Atlas Copco, Volvo, and SEB should lead on exporters' USD/SEK tailwinds and widening NIM from higher yields. Boliden (mining leverage to gold/copper) is a specific long into strength. Ericsson and Hexagon (12% tech/telecom) face headwinds from NASDAQ flat (-0.31%) and yield pressure; avoid here. H&M (EUR/SEK down -0.38%) faces margin pressure. ADX below 18 signals low trend conviction—reduce position sizing by 30%. Key event: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET could spark volatility if data surprises. Trade the ceasefire bounce but respect mean-reversion risk from Stochastic overbought conditions.
drivers: Iran-US ceasefire deal sparks global risk-on rally; DAX +1.28%, CAC +1.18%
Gold +3.40% and copper +0.96% lift mining/materials; USD/SEK weakness offset by commodity strength
OMX30 +8.1% above 200-MA in bull market but weak ADX (8.6) limits conviction; Stochastic overbought at K=82.1 signals mean-reversion risk

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield surge to 4.487% (+54bp) widens net interest margin expectations; risk-on sentiment supports credit risk perception.
USD/SEK weakness (-0.66%) partially offset by geopolitical risk premium and strong European momentum; export competitiveness supported.
Gold +3.40% and copper +0.96% provide direct revenue uplift; Boliden particularly sensitive to precious metals and base metals pricing.
NASDAQ flat (+0.31%) and US 10Y yields elevated compress DCF valuations; risk-off in growth names despite global risk-on backdrop.
Higher yields (4.487%) increase refinancing and discount rate headwinds; property valuations under pressure.
EUR/SEK weakness (-0.38%) pressures H&M sourcing costs; oil weakness (-5.11%) only modest offset to broader yield/confidence headwind.

watchList.reason: Gold +3.40% and copper +0.96% deliver direct revenue drivers; commodity strength likely to persist on geopolitical risk premium.
Industrial bellwether benefits from risk-on European momentum, moderate USD/SEK tailwind, and cyclical recovery narrative on ceasefire relief.
NIM expansion from 4.487% 10Y yield supports earnings; risk-on environment favors bank sentiment.
NASDAQ tepid, yields pressuring DCF; growth narrative fragile despite risk-on backdrop.
EUR/SEK weakness (-0.38%) directly pressures sourcing margins; lack of offsetting tailwinds.

keyLevels.pivotWatch: 3123 (Friday close, key pivot for momentum shift)

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—potential volatility catalyst if data diverges from expectations.
topRisk: Stochastic overbought (K=82.1) and weak ADX (8.6) flag mean-reversion risk; any disappointing US data or escalation in geopolitical tensions could trigger sharp pullback. Oil weakness (-5.11%) may not sustain risk-on if ceasefire narrative reverses.
technicalSummary: Weak trend: ADX 8.6 (below 18 threshold) reduces confidence to Medium; cut position magnitude by 30%
Overbought: Stochastic K=82.1, D=51.2—mean reversion risk
MACD: Below signal, negative histogram (-2.371)—momentum warning
